key_themes: Analyst Buy/Outperform ratings with ~20% average upside to ~$93 PT
Q1 2026 earnings preview and expected EPS growth
Rejection of low-ball mini-tender offer to protect shareholders
Stable dividends and preferred stock actions
Solid variable investment income outlook ahead of May earnings
summary: MetLife (MET) is generating mildly positive sentiment as it approaches its Q1 2026 earnings release on May 6. Analysts maintain a consensus Overweight/Buy stance with an average 12-month price target near $93 (versus recent trading around $78), even after several modest PT reductions. The company’s preview of $475–525M in Q1 variable investment income, expected EPS growth, routine dividend confirmations, and proactive warning against a deeply discounted mini-tender offer from Potemkin Limited are reinforcing stable investor confidence.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/MET/forecast/)[[2]](https://investor.metlife.com/resources/stock-info/historical-stock-quote/)
sources: 15+ recent news articles, press releases, and analyst notes, 10+ relevant X posts discussing $MET financial updates and ratings
